lines.text: Tucker here, East Side perimeter, near the Atwood commercial strip. We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the phone lines are absolutely jammed — I've never heard Quinn's crew scrambling like this. Something's different about tonight.

Madison-wide cumulative at {{CLOCK}}: forty-three confirmed kills. That's eight ahead of this point last year. Year-over-year delta is plus nineteen percent. East Side alone is carrying twenty-two of those — that's fifty-one percent of the night's activity in a single neighborhood.

I'm repositioning north toward Schenk's Corners. The activity pattern is clustering tighter than usual. Normally we see dispersal by this hour. Tonight it's — hold on, there's a group moving toward the ShopRite loading dock and I need to adjust angle — okay. They're armed, looks like three of them, moving methodically. That's not random.

Neighborhood breakdown: East Side fifty-one percent, Capitol Square notional perimeter showing nineteen percent, Williamson Street's holding at fourteen percent but their barricade — I can see it from here — is still intact. That's stabilizing their numbers.

The caller volume is spiking. I'm getting audio from the studio and people are asking questions I don't have answers for yet. That never happens at this hour. Caroline was running weather — she said something about pressure systems and I think she meant something else.

Weapons trade analysis: firearms percentage is tracking at forty-six percent of incidents. Bladed's at twenty-four percent. That's consistent with 2168 at this point, so economically we're not seeing new — wait, hold on, I'm getting feedback on my audio pack, my laptop just —

tickerItems.text: Phone lines at capacity. Listener caller volume highest on record for hour 4.
Williamson St barricade holding. Capitol perimeter at 19% activity. No breach attempts.
Field report cut due to equipment failure. Tucker status TBD.
